info.title: Supergrid Web API Specification
info.description:
Note: the maximum length of the query string is limited to 2048 characters by default. The limit can be increased by changing maxQueryString and maxQueryStringLength options in the configuration file.

## Errors Logging

Error logs can be accessed at the following URL: [/GetErrors](../GetErrors)

### Supported Parameters:

- [/GetErrors](../GetErrors) provides error logs for the last 7 days.

- [/GetErrors/24](../GetErrors/24) provides error logs for the last 24 hours. If the number is greater than 168, error logs for the last week will be retrieved.

- [/GetErrors/id1027](../GetErrors/id1027) provides error log details where the ID is equal to 1027.

- [/GetErrors/idAfter1027](../GetErrors/idAfter1027) provides error log details where the ID is greater than 1027 but within the last week.

- [/GetErrors/m30](../GetErrors/m30) provides error logs for the last 30 minutes. If the number is greater than 10080, error logs for the last week will be retrieved.

- [/GetErrors/d15-07-2024](../GetErrors/d15-07-2024) provides error logs created on a specific date (15-07-2024, day-month-year format).

### Optional Parameter: `environment`

The `environment` parameter can be used to filter error logs specific to a particular environment. This parameter helps in tracking and analyzing errors based on the environment but is optional for retrieving error logs. Setting this parameter will only show error logs originating from the given envrionment.

 Supported values include:

- **Web**
- **WebMaster**
- **BackgroundWorker**
- **InsightPlusSyncService**

### Example Request with `environment` Parameter:
```http
GET /GetErrors?environment=BackgroundWorker
```

parameters.name: timeUnit
parameters.description: Time unit of assignment allocations aggregation. By default allocations aggregated by <see cref="F:SG.Api.Model.TimeUnit.Day">day</see>. <see cref="F:SG.Api.Model.TimeUnit.Project">Project</see> is also possible. APIKey.description:
API authenticates an account by using the API token generated by the user account. A user with 'Use API' global role permission can request API token from profile screen. The access token must be sent in an HTTP authorization header with every API call. It's recommended to send API requests over HTTPS. API requests over HTTP are not safe and restricted by default. If you want to allow API access for HTTP - IsApiOverHttpEnabled flag must be set to 'true' in the configuration file. An api token represents a users and the users permissions. It must not be published or shared among multiple users.

Header example:

Authorization: Bearer 1-8051a5c4-3e6e-481e-9dd6-2f474d6061e3

OAuth.description:
API authenticates an account by using the OAuth JWT token generated by the oauth token endpoint at /oauth2/token. The access token must be sent in an HTTP authorization header with every API call. It's recommended to send API requests over HTTPS. API requests over HTTP are not safe and restricted by default. If you want to allow API access for HTTP - IsApiOverHttpEnabled flag must be set to 'true' in the configuration file. The token endpoint will never be accessible over HTTP for security reasons. An api token represents a users and the users permissions. It must not be published or shared among multiple users.

Header example:

Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAxLzA0L3htbGRzaWctbW9yZSNobWFjLXNoYTI1NiIsImtpZCI6IjMyMiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJodHRwOi8vc2NoZW1hcy54bWxzb2FwLm9yZy93cy8yMDA1LzA1L2lkZW50aXR5L2NsYWltcy9uYW1lIjoiYWRtaW4iLCJodHRwOi8vc2NoZW1hcy54bWxzb2FwLm9yZy93cy8yMDA1LzA1L2lkZW50aXR5L2NsYWltcy9uYW1laWRlbnRpZmllciI6IjIiLCJuYmYiOjE2MTUyOTY3MzEsImV4cCI6MTYxNTM4MzEzMSwiaXNzIjoiaHR0cDovL2xvY2FsaG9zdCIsImF1ZCI6IjMyMiJ9.8Fhusr-wrtzKSscNiFJ--6ySyj6O3m8XuXpBbAuIUAY

Token endpoint Curl request example:

curl --location --request POST 'https://domain.com/oauth2/token' --header 'Accept: application/json' --header 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' --data-urlencode 'grant_type=password' --data-urlencode 'password=Password@1' --data-urlencode 'username=admin' --data-urlencode 'scope=SG'

tags.name: Assignments
tags.description:
## Compression Users can work with allocations in compressed and uncompressed format. Compressed collection includes two types of groups: - Group starting from positive number and including two elements. For example (3, 8) means the next 3 elements equals to 8, uncompressed collection: (8, 8, 8). - Group starting from negative number and including two or more elements. For example (-3, 7, 8, 7) means the next 3 elements not compressed, uncompressed collection: (7, 8, 7). 
Example:

compressed collection: (5, 8, -4, 5, 6, 7, 6, 5, 0, -2, 8, 7)
- group (5, 8) uncompressed: (8, 8, 8, 8, 8)
- group (-4, 5, 6, 7, 6) uncompressed: (5, 6, 7, 6)
- group (5, 0) uncompressed: (0, 0, 0, 0, 0)
- group (-2, 8, 7) uncompressed: (8, 7)
uncompressed collection: (8, 8, 8, 8, 8, 5, 6, 7, 6, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 8, 7)

## Weekends Settings Weekends settings can be configured in general settings. The sample code for validating if day is a weekend is given below. 
int dayOfWeek = 0; // 0 - Sunday, 6 - Saturday
bool isWeekend = (Weekends & (1 << dayOfWeek)) > 0.

tags.name: Subscriptions
tags.description:
Users can subscribe to different events with API using endpoint url for each subscription. When a new webhook subscription is created, the system will send a test request to endpoint url and verify that the endpoint is active and can receive data. User's application receives the request and replies with HTTP 200 status code to confirm receipt of data. Any other status code will be treated as a failure. When an event occurs in the system, each subscription for the event will trigger an HTTP POST request to the url specified. In case, a client application cannot accept notification, the system will retry again in 15 minutes 4 times. After that the sytem will try to send the notification every hour. If it still fails after 24 hours no new notification will be sent to the subscribers until the url has been reset via the API. ## Event Types and Filters Each subscription has an event type and filter type. Filter type is optional. The following table lists the available filter options for each event type. **Event: ProjectCreate**
A project is created. **Event: ProjectFixedFieldsUpdate**
An out of the box project setting is modified. Example: Name, Project Security Group
***Filters:*** ProjectId **Event: ProjectDelete**
A project is deleted.
***Filters:*** ProjectId **Event: ProjectTimestampsUpdate**
The project update timestamp is modified.
***Filters:*** ProjectId **Event: ResourceCreate**
A resource is created. **Event: ResourceFixedFieldsUpdate**
An out of the box resource setting is modified. Example: Resource Name, Resource Security Group.
***Filters:*** ResourceId **Event: ResourceDelete**
A resource is deleted.
***Filters:*** ResourceId **Event: ResourceTimestampsUpdate**
The resource update timestamp is modified.
***Filters:*** ResourceId **Event: TaskCreate**
A task is created.
***Filters:*** ProjectId **Event: TaskUpdate**
A task is updated.
***Filters:*** ProjectId **Event: TaskDelete**
A task is deleted.
***Filters:*** ProjectId **Event: AssignmentCreate**
An assignment is created.
***Filters:*** ProjectId, ResourceId **Event: AssignmentUpdate**
Assignment allocation is updated.
***Filters:*** ProjectId, ResourceId **Event: AssignmentDelete**
An assignment is deleted.
***Filters:*** ProjectId, ResourceId **Event: ResourceNetCapacityUpdate**
Resource net capacity is updated.
***Filters:*** ResourceId **Event: TimesheetCreate**
A timesheet is created. Can be filtered by timeheet user and timesheet approver.
***Filters:*** ResourceId, TimesheetApproverResourceId **Event: TimesheetDelete**
A timesheet is deleted. Can be filtered by timeheet user and timesheet approver.
***Filters:*** ResourceId, TimesheetApproverResourceId **Event: TimesheetStatusUpdate**
Timesheet status changes. Can be filtered by timeheet user and timesheet approver.
***Filters:*** ResourceId, TimesheetApproverResourceId **Event: ResourceRequestCreate**
A resource request is created. Can be filtered by resource, resource manager and the resource initiating the request.
***Filters:*** ResourceId, ResourceRequestOwnerResourceId, ResourceRequestRequestedByResourceId **Event: ResourceRequestStatusUpdate**
Resource request status changes. Can be filtered by resource, resource manager and the resource initiating the request.
***Filters:*** ResourceId, ResourceRequestOwnerResourceId, ResourceRequestRequestedByResourceId **Event: ResourceRequestDelete**
A resource request is deleted. Can be filtered by resource, resource manager and the resource initiating the request.
***Filters:*** ResourceId, ResourceRequestOwnerResourceId, ResourceRequestRequestedByResourceId **Event: ProjectCustomFieldValuesUpdate**
A project custom field value changes.
***Filters:*** ProjectId, CustomFieldId **Event: ResourceCustomFieldValuesUpdate**
A resource custom field value changes.
***Filters:*** ResourceId, CustomFieldId **Event: AssignmentCustomFieldValuesUpdate**
An assignment custom field value changes.
***Filters:*** ProjectId, ResourceId, CustomFieldId **Event: CustomFieldCreate**
A custom field is created **Event: CustomFieldUpdate**
A custom field is updated.
***Filters:*** CustomFieldId **Event: CustomFieldDelete**
A custom field is deleted.
***Filters:*** CustomFieldId # Webhook Security We recommend verifying each webhook request to make sure it comes from SuperGrid and not altered or corrupted during transmission. You can do that by validating the webhook signature coming with each webhook. ## Generating Secret Key A shared secret key is automatically generated and sent back to client as a response when a new create subscription request is made. The secret key is unique per subscription. Client should store the Id and secret key pair in their app to validate incoming notification. The secret key is sent as a base 64 encoded string. ## Webhook Signature All notifications matching the subscription will contain a header with timestamp and HMAC SHA256 signature. The signature contains a timestamp and the signature hash. The timestamp is in UTC and denoted as "t". The second part is the signature hash as base 64 string which is denoted as "sha256".

**X-SGT5-Webhook-Signature**: t=637087142357899885,sha256=/mDmLXux6Hxkuue8lrJ/5GlnP5wOVqrYPp+8XLMV6Ac= ## Comparing Signature The signature can be generated using the follow procedure. - Parse the timestamp from header value. The timestamp is the integer value after "t=". - Prepare a string message with timestamp and append a dot followed by the request body json content.
message = [timestamp].[body_content] - Compute HMAC with the SHA256 hash function using the message and secret key associated with subscription. - Convert the computed hash bytes to base 64 string using UTF-8 encoding. - Extract the hash value from header. The hash value is text after "sha256=". - Compare the computed hash with the hash received in header. ## Code Sample The SuperGrid client SDK has a helper method that you can directly call to validate the signature. The sample code for validating is given below. 
public static DateTime Validate(string sgt5WebhookSignature, string secretKey, string content)
{
    if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(sgt5WebhookSignature))
    {
        throw new ArgumentException($"{nameof(sgt5WebhookSignature)} must not be null or whitespace.");
    }

    if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(secretKey))
    {
        throw new ArgumentException($"{nameof(secretKey)} must not be null or whitespace.");
    }

    if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(content))
    {
        throw new ArgumentException($"{nameof(content)} must not be null or whitespace.");
    }

    var match = Regex.Match(sgt5WebhookSignature, "^t=(\d+),sha256=(.+)$");

    if (!match.Success)
    {
        throw new Exception($"The signature pattern ({sgt5WebhookSignature}) does not match.");
    }

    var timestamp = long.Parse(match.Groups[1].Value);
    var signature = match.Groups[2].Value;

    var message = $"{timestamp}.{content}";

    using (var hasher = new HMACSHA256(Convert.FromBase64String(secretKey)))
    {
        var computedSignature = Convert.ToBase64String(hasher.ComputeHash(Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(message)));

        if (computedSignature != signature)
        {
            throw new Exception("Signature does not match.");
        }
    }

    return new DateTime(timestamp, DateTimeKind.Utc);
}

tags.name: Reports
tags.description:
The Reports API provides endpoints to retrieve report data in a flat format.

**Steps:**
1. Use `/Reports` to retrieve available report IDs.
2. Use `/Reports/PrepareData` to queue the report for processing.
3. Use `/Reports/QueueItems` to check if the report is ready.
4. Once ready, use `/Reports/RetrieveData` to download the raw report data.

**Note:** The response from the Retrieve Data endpoint may be large depending on the report's size and configuration. Clients should be prepared to handle large payloads efficiently.
